title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
District Attorney Isaacs informs Ellery and Vane that the victim, Brad, was holding a chess piece when he was killed, which fell from his hand before he was tied up. Isaacs notes that an unfinished chess game exists in Brad's study. The group moves from the grass, where Dr. Rumsen is examining the corpse, to a small, circular, rustic gazebo near a totem pole. Inside the gazebo, they find a table, two chairs, and a large reddish-brown bloodstain on the floor. Ellery discovers a large capital letter "T" painted in fresh blood on the wooden floor behind the table. Professor Yadley notes the strange combination of Native American, Christian, and phallic symbols present at the scene.

Ellery examines a long-handled stone pipe carved with Neptune and his trident near the bloodstain. Although Officer Vorn states the pipe belongs to Brad because of his fingerprints, Ellery notices the pipe contains unburnt tobacco cut into cubes. Ellery collects a sample of this tobacco in a glassine envelope, theorizing that if the cubed tobacco does not match Brad's cigar box, it will serve as a clue identifying the killer.

Ellery hypothesizes that Brad carried a red chess pawn from the house to the gazebo for a specific reason and was attacked while there. Dr. Rumsen returns to report that Brad has been dead for approximately fourteen hours, suggesting the crime occurred in the dark around 10:00 PM. Rumsen also notes that the killer struck Brad directly on the head and observes a strawberry-shaped birthmark on the victim's right knee. As the group leaves the gazebo, Officer Vorn reminds Ellery that he has information to share.
